scholarships.organization: Thurgood Marshall College Fund (TMCF) — Open Scholarships Portal
scholarships.description: Howard is a TMCF member school, so Mason can create a TMCF Scholar profile and get auto-matched to dozens of corporate-sponsored scholarships (Wells Fargo Access/Gap Scholarships, Acushnet Company Scholarship, and more) that rotate through TMCF's portal each cycle.
requirements: Attend a TMCF member HBCU (Howard qualifies)
Create a profile on the TMCF Scholarship Portal
Individual sponsor awards vary — filter by business/finance and sophomore standing once logged in
GPA thresholds vary by sponsor, typically 2.5–3.0+ (Mason's 3.7 clears most)
scholarships.deadlineNote: New wave expected ~September 2026; two 2026 waves already closed (Jan 16 & Apr 24)

scholarships.description: $10,000 awards for non-medical STEM or business majors, with an HBCU/HSI-specific track. New Jersey is an eligible state, both as Mason's home state and as an American Water operating territory.
requirements: Full-time undergraduate in a non-medical STEM or business-related major
Minimum 3.0 GPA
Reside or attend college in an eligible state (NJ included)
HBCU/HSI track applies directly to Howard students
scholarships.deadlineNote: Historically due Jan 31; 2026-27 cycle listed as closed/tentative — watch for a fall/winter 2026 reopening

scholarships.description: A network of Black corporate executives runs this flagship scholarship building a pipeline of Black business talent. Strong fit: it explicitly names "Rising Sophomore" as an eligible class year and prefers Business majors.
requirements: Full-time enrollment at an accredited 4-year college
Minimum 3.0 GPA
Rising Sophomore, Junior, Senior, or Graduate standing
Business, Engineering, or Technology major preferred
Need-based: FAFSA Submission Summary + tuition bill required
Essays, video prompts, business case responses, transcript, recommendation letter, resume
scholarships.deadlineNote: Estimated — annual deadline is typically March 1; the 2026 cycle already closed

scholarships.organization: NABA National Scholarship Program (National Association of Black Accountants)
scholarships.description: NABA's foundation funds 150+ scholarships a year ($2,500–$10,000) for Black/minority undergraduates in accounting, finance, or business — one of the best-matched programs for Mason's major.
requirements: Ethnic minority undergraduate business major (finance qualifies)
Paid NABA member for the current fiscal year
Overall GPA of 3.3+ (3.5 for accounting majors) — Mason's 3.7 clears this
Full-time enrollment (12+ semester hours), freshman through first-year senior
Selection weighs academic achievement, leadership, service, and engagement with NABA
scholarships.deadlineNote: Estimated — recent cycles clustered around late June; confirm exact date on nabainc.org

scholarships.organization: Congressional Black Caucus Foundation — CBC Spouses Education Scholarship
scholarships.description: CBCF's flagship undergraduate award for academically talented Black/African American students. Howard/DC sits in a CBC member's district (Rep. Eleanor Holmes Norton), which likely satisfies the district-residency requirement.
requirements: U.S. citizen or legal permanent resident
Reside or attend school in a CBC member's congressional district (Howard/DC likely qualifies)
Full-time undergraduate, graduate, or doctoral enrollment
Minimum 2.5 GPA
Demonstrated leadership and community involvement
2-part online application, 3-minute video statement, 1 recommendation letter, transcript, resume
scholarships.deadlineNote: 2026 cycle ran Jan 5 – Mar 27 (already closed); watch for the Fall 2026 announcement of the 2027 cycle

scholarships.organization: National Black MBA Association (NBMBAA) Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Funds undergraduate and MBA scholarships ($1,000–$10,000) for Black students in business fields. Likely requires junior standing, so this is a bookmark for Mason's 2027-28 junior year rather than right now.
requirements: Paid NBMBAA membership
College junior, senior, or MBA student (rising sophomores likely not yet eligible)
Pursuing a business-related degree
Full-time enrollment, U.S. citizen or permanent resident
Community service/leadership involvement
scholarships.deadlineNote: Not yet eligible as a rising sophomore — revisit for junior year; 2026 deadline was May 29

scholarships.organization: LeCount R. Davis, CFP® Endowed Scholarship (CFP Board Center for Financial Planning)
scholarships.description: A scholarship specifically for Black/African American students pursuing CFP® certification. Requires enrollment in a CFP Board Registered Program — confirm whether Howard's finance curriculum has this track before applying.
requirements: Self-identifies as Black or African American
Enrolled in (or seeking) a CFP Board Registered Program
Demonstrated merit and financial need
Committed to pursuing CFP certification and a financial planning career
scholarships.deadlineNote: Deadline not published — check cfp.net directly; cycles typically run in spring

scholarships.description: Not a cash scholarship, but a highly-regarded paid internship and professional-development pipeline for high-achieving Black, Hispanic, and Native American undergraduates targeting Wall Street and finance careers. Interns average $10,000 for the summer with ~80% converting to full-time offers.
requirements: Undergraduate (rising sophomores through rising seniors typically apply)
Black, Hispanic, or Native American identity
Interest in finance, Wall Street, real estate, or related corporate tracks
Competitive application: resume, interviews, assessments
scholarships.deadlineNote: Rolling/track-based — summer internship applications typically open fall/winter prior; check seo-usa.org

scholarships.description: A paid internship (not a scholarship) placing Black, Hispanic, and Native American college students into corporate roles, with many finance/business placements — a useful funding complement now that Mason is internship-eligible as a rising sophomore.
requirements: Full-time undergraduate with at least one summer remaining before graduation
Minimum cumulative GPA of 2.8
Business is a named target field
INROADS-formatted resume, unofficial transcript
Completion of INROADS Interview Guidance and Preparation Session (IGPS)
scholarships.deadlineNote: Rolling — varies by region and corporate partner; apply directly at inroads.org

scholarships.organization: 100 Black Men of America — National Scholarship Program
scholarships.description: Awards of $1,000–$5,000 for full-time college undergraduates. Eligibility appears restricted to 100 Black Men mentees, Collegiate 100® members, or program affiliates — worth connecting with a local NJ chapter to qualify.
requirements: 100 Black Men mentee, Collegiate 100 member, or affiliate
Full-time college undergraduate
Minimum 2.5 GPA
scholarships.deadlineNote: Varies by local chapter — check 100blackmen.org and NJ-area chapters

scholarships.organization: Howard University Alumni Club of New Jersey (HUAC-NJ)
scholarships.description: The Howard alumni club serving New Jersey runs mentorship and scholarship efforts for Bison students — a natural, high-affinity fit given Mason is both an NJ resident and a Howard student.
requirements: Likely NJ residency and/or Howard enrollment
Specific criteria not published — contact the club directly
scholarships.deadlineNote: Unknown — reach out to the club directly for current opportunities

scholarships.organization: Community Foundation of New Jersey — Scholarship Universal Application
scholarships.description: CFNJ administers roughly 140 separate scholarship funds for NJ students through one universal application; some funds support business/finance-track or continuing (non-senior) students.
requirements: Typically requires NJ residency or NJ high school graduation
Eligibility varies by underlying fund
Some funds are renewable/open to continuing college students
scholarships.deadlineNote: Varies by fund — portal typically opens each spring

scholarships.description: Merit-based scholarship for financial-services students actively promoting diversity within the financial planning profession.
requirements: Financial services student or professional
Demonstrated engagement raising awareness of financial planning in diverse communities, or expanding opportunities for underrepresented groups in the field
GPA/class-year requirements not detailed — verify on FPA's site
scholarships.deadlineNote: Not specified — recent recognitions occurred in September, suggesting a mid-year cycle; confirm on site

scholarships.description: Not a cash scholarship, but a significant CFA Program exam fee reduction (from roughly $1,200+ down to $600) for university students — useful to know about now for when Mason eventually pursues the CFA charter.
requirements: Current full-time or part-time university student
Must meet CFA Program enrollment requirements
Not currently registered for a CFA exam, not awaiting results
scholarships.deadlineNote: Four application windows per year tied to each exam cycle — check cfainstitute.org for the next open window

scholarships.description: A large, well-established scholarship ($25,000 awards) for students demonstrating financial need and resilience. Eligibility for currently-enrolled sophomores versus only incoming freshmen is ambiguous in published materials — verify directly before applying.
requirements: Household AGI roughly $55,000–$65,000 or lower (varies by source)
Minimum 2.0 GPA
U.S. citizen
Co-curricular/community service involvement, demonstrated integrity and perseverance
Verify whether continuing college sophomores qualify — some tracks may be HS-senior-entry only
scholarships.deadlineNote: Historically a Dec 1 – Mar 1 window — confirm whether a continuing-student track exists

scholarships.description: Easy-entry scholarship open to any student, no essay required, with rolling monthly winners — low odds but zero-cost and takes minutes to enter.
requirements: Any U.S. student
No essay, no GPA minimum specified
scholarships.deadlineNote: Rolling monthly; 2026 cycle's final entry was listed as June 30, 2026 — check for the next cycle

scholarships.organization: Sallie Monthly Scholarship for College Sophomores
scholarships.description: A $2,000 monthly scholarship specifically open to college sophomores — directly matches Mason's current class year, no essay or profile required beyond simple entry.
requirements: Enrolled college sophomore
No essay required
